MAJOR REQUIREMENTS ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"198b6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>As organisms that sequester carbon and convert solar energy into oxygen, sugar and other usable forms, plant are a primary source of food and myriad biomaterials on the planet and function as an important buffer against climate change. The Plant Biology major focuses on fundamental aspects of how plants function as organisms and interact with their environment, and the use of this knowledge to address global challenges. A wide variety of scientific disciplines are integrated within the Plant Biology major, including physiology, cell and molecular biology, development, genetics and genomics.</p><p><br></p><p>The <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Plant Biology</a> major consists of a biological sciences core covering the general principles of biology plus three plant-specific classes dealing with advanced aspects of plant biology including physiology, development, and anatomy. Electives allow students to tailor the degree to suit their interests. Independent research in a laboratory setting is a requirement, and students in the Plant Biology B.S. major are guaranteed this opportunity. Because of the value of plants as a model system for research in molecular genetics, cell biology, and biochemistry, Plant Biology also can make an excellent minor or second major for student in these fields.</p>","groupId":"1b8b6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"CAREER ALTERNATIVES","groupId":"1d8b6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"GENERAL INFORMATION","groupId":"1f8b6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":8,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p><strong style=\"color: rgb(78, 14, 80);\">Group 3 and Group 4: </strong>Please note that students may be able to meet major requirements with different combinations of courses. Please visit the major for more details and advising.</p>","groupId":"218b6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":9,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>At the time of <a href=\"https://www.ucdavis.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer/transfer-admission-guarantee\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">TAG</a> submission the College of Biological Sciences requires a minimum <strong>3.20 </strong>cumulative GPA and at least a <strong>2.50</strong> GPA in each series with no grades less than a C for all majors to receive a Transfer Admission Guarantee.</p>","groupId":"688e6f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":7,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>This is a competitive major and is subject to the <a href=\"https://www.ucdavis.edu/admissions/undergraduate/transfer/selective-major-requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Selective Major Review</a> process. Students need to take all courses for a letter grade with no grade less than C and complete all major preparatory course requirements by the end of spring term prior to their fall enrollment. All students must meet a 2.8 GPA cumulative GPA for admission.</p><p> </p><p>Important Information:</p><p><br></p><p>You must complete the courses below and maintain a<strong> 2.50 </strong>GPA in each series:</p><p>●     Biological Sciences (BIS) 002A/B/C</p><p>●     Chemistry (CHE) 002A/B/C or 004A/B/C</p><p>●     Mathematics (MAT) 017A/B/C or 021A/B</p><p><br></p><p>Highly Recommended Courses (not required for admission)</p><p><br></p><p>●     Organic Chemistry 8A/B or 118A/B/C</p><p>●     Physics 7A/B/C</p><p><br></p><p>Visit Department agreements as needed</p><p><br></p><p>Contact your UC Davis Advisor for questions (<a href=\"https://ua.ucdavis.edu/portal/find-my-advisor\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Find my advisor</a>)</p><p><br></p><p>Note:</p><p><strong>Advanced Placement (AP) and International Baccalaureate (IB) Examination:</strong></p><p>AP and IB examination credit policies are detailed in the UC Davis <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/catalog/GenCat20252026.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">General Catalog</a>. Quick reference charts for <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/APCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">AP</a> and <a href=\"https://local-resources.ucdavis.edu/local_resources/docs/APIBCharts/IBCurrent.pdf\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">IB</a>.</p>","groupId":"d7d20098-817b-4209-b46d-019a4a88baf1","position":5,"area":"General"}]
